description:
Description:Gray and “salmon” colored heron smaller than nearby Great Blue Herons, and slightly larger than Snowy Egrets. First found standing still on one leg face us. Marginal
wing coverts gray-reddish/salmon. Primaries and secondaries gray. Head, neck and body dirty reddish-gray. Eyes yellow, bill and legs black. Behavior:When first found it was standing still in the water near the muddy shoreline. After about 40 minutes the bird began to become active and starting walking and periodically lifting its wings as it hunted small fish (shad sp.?). About 2.5 hours later it became VERY active walking and “sprinting” back and forth, chasing fish and actively (and often) raising it wings. Note: Later we noticed that the nearby Snowy Egrets were feeding in a similar fashion…which we thought odd as we thought only Reddish Egrets fed this way. We were on the dam of Jackson Reservoir at the outlet canal looking north with the sun to our backs. I noticed this bird and thought, “Reddish Egret or Sandhill Crane?” Quickly realized that even standing on one leg it did not have the posture of a crane (more upright and less tail feather “fluff”).  Below eBird checklists contain this species seen at different times during the day:https://ebird.org/checklist/S7376199911:36am – 1:10pm from the top of the dam and down onto the mudflatshttps://ebird.org/checklist/S737537431:47-3:30pm
from the campground on the west side of the reservoir
photographer: Photographer: , Joey Kellner, Joey Kellner, Joey Kellner
